author: codex
scope: Codex-only relation endpoint-shape rule-or-data triage; deterministic local inspection plus this single prep artifact; no provider-backed commands, generated-output commands, durable data edits, or code edits

recent_work_not_repeated: relation cleanup batches037-044 for timeline, published_by, implemented_by, related_route, and part_of_program buckets
current_checkpoint_context.inspection_method: Read-only SQLite queries against data/canonical.db using the current RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES table from packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts, plus jq and rg inspection of existing canonical JSONL, submission journals, and source blocks.

remaining_bucket_counts.triage_note: All current exact endpoint-shape buckets are singletons. After excluding recently handled timeline, published_by, implemented_by, related_route, part_of_program, and validator-rule lanes, has_treatment claim->treatment_component is the clearest next data cleanup: the source-backed treatment and claim are both valid records, but the relation uses the wrong relation kind/direction for a claim subject.

recommended_data_cleanup.why_data_cleanup: The current has_treatment endpoint contract intentionally scopes treatments to projects, corridors, and routes. This row instead uses a claim as the subject. The source evidence supports a treatment component and a source-stated claim about that treatment, not a claim owning a treatment. The recently admitted has_claim treatment_component->claim shape preserves the relationship without widening has_treatment.
recommended_data_cleanup.why_not_validator_rule_patch: Allowing has_treatment claim->treatment_component would make claims containers for treatment records and blur the pipeline's ownership boundary: claims are assertion records, while treatment_component records should attach to project/corridor/route scope or carry their own claim context via has_claim. The inverse valid shape already exists, so this is not an ontology gap.

candidate_submissions_to_retire.retire_reason: Malformed direction/kind: the claim record is used as the has_treatment subject.

replacement_direction.recommended_label: New elevator at Livonia Av & Rockaway Parkway has source claim

recommended_data_cleanup.existing_replacement_check: Read-only relation lookup found no existing relation involving these two endpoints except relation_accessibility-has-treatment-elevator, so replacement would not duplicate an already materialized edge.

representative_records.recommended_action: retire relation_accessibility-has-treatment-elevator and replace it with has_claim treatment_component->claim

subject.display_name: New Elevator at Livonia Av & Rockaway Parkway

object.display_name: New elevator at Livonia Av & Rockaway Parkway

evidence.source_quote: Welcome back to a new elevator! Thank you for your patience during construction.

representative_records.triage_read: The page text supports a treatment component at Livonia Av & Rockaway Parkway and a claim/description about the new elevator. It does not support making the claim the scope owner for a has_treatment edge.

Not selected. This is also malformed, but it appears to confuse treatment and metric semantics. It should remain a separate future data cleanup and is not evidence for widening has_treatment to entity or metric endpoints.
Not selected. This may need event-to-project/treatment restructuring, but event subjects should not be admitted to has_treatment just to clear one row.

Not selected. Entity service operated-by entity may be semantically plausible, but the cited evidence is only a Paratransit Report agenda line in an NYCT/Bus committee context. That is too thin for a validator rule patch in this bucket.
Not selected. This remains a data error after the entity->corridor rule patch; the object is an agency entity while the evidence says the ACE/ABLE program began on buses across routes and boroughs.

source_and_canonical_evidence_summary: Canonical relation relation_accessibility-has-treatment-elevator comes from accepted submission sub_e9da8479c21e0686 in source meeting_doc_109976.
The relation's subject endpoint is canonical claim claim_new-elevator-livonia-rockaway, whose payload claim_text is 'Welcome back to a new elevator! Thank you for your patience during construction.'

The relation's object endpoint is canonical treatment_component treatment_elevator-livonia-rockaway, whose payload describes a new elevator installed at the Livonia Av & Rockaway Parkway L subway station entrance.
The source evidence blocks are a page-13 image description showing the elevator/signage at Livonia Av & Rockaway Parkway L and a page-13 heading, 'Advancing Systemwide Accessibility'.

A read-only lookup of relations involving either endpoint found only the malformed relation_accessibility-has-treatment-elevator; no existing has_claim treatment_component->claim replacement was present.

selected_source_blocks.raw_text: A photograph of a subway station entrance. In the foreground, a man in a light blue shirt and dark pants is seated in a wheelchair, smiling. To his left, a woman in a black jacket and red bag is pushing a red stroller with a purple bag. Behind them, a man in a blue patterned blazer and grey trousers is walking towards the camera. In the background, there is a glass elevator with a sign that says "Welcome back to a new elevator! Thank you for your patience during construction." Above the elevator, there is a sign for "Exit" with accessibility icons and "Livonia Av & Rockaway Parkway L". A yellow arrow points to the "Exit" sign with the text "Increased Appeal".

validator_boundary.interpretation: The treatment component can carry the claim context through has_claim, while has_treatment remains reserved for attaching treatments to scope records.

non_goals: Do not retire, replace, or edit canonical data as part of this prep.
Do not apply code or validator rule patches in this artifact-only lane.
Do not rerun materialize, export-jsonl, full validate, DB publish, write, ask, schema, ontology-normalize, identity-review, canonicalize, campaign dedup, or any provider/profile-backed command.
Do not repeat next20's operates_on_corridor entity->corridor validator rule patch or recent relation044/identity076 work.
Do not pick another timeline, published_by, implemented_by, related_route, or part_of_program data batch in this next21 triage.
Do not widen has_treatment to claim subjects, entity subjects, event subjects, metric_claim objects, claim objects, source subjects, or source_gap endpoints.
Do not infer a project/corridor/route scope for the elevator from this cleanup unless a later data pass separately finds source-backed scope evidence.

commands_run_read_only: sqlite3 -json 'file:data/canonical.db?mode=ro' '<current endpoint-shape bucket inventory using RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES known kinds only>'

sqlite3 'file:data/canonical.db?mode=ro' '.schema evidence_refs' && sqlite3 'file:data/canonical.db?mode=ro' '.schema record_submissions' && sqlite3 'file:data/canonical.db?mode=ro' '.schema record_sources'
sqlite3 -json 'file:data/canonical.db?mode=ro' '<current endpoint-shape issue rows with endpoints, payloads, evidence, and submissions>'
sqlite3 -json 'file:data/canonical.db?mode=ro' '<canonical selected relation, claim, and treatment records>'
